description_long: This is a wooden fence composed of two vertical posts and three horizontal rails, with a distinctive diagonal brace running from the lower left to the upper right. The wood appears smooth and evenly colored, with a light brown finish typical of treated lumber. The diagonal brace is constructed from the same material and is rendered with a pixelated or stepped edge, giving it a stylized appearance. The top of the fence is flat, and the posts extend slightly above the uppermost rail, providing a simple and functional design. There are no visible decorative elements or embellishments, and the structure is solid and utilitarian. This type of fence is commonly used in urban parks, gardens, or as a boundary marker along sidewalks and pathways.
description: A simple wooden fence with three horizontal rails and a diagonal brace, finished in light brown. The design is functional and unadorned, with flat-topped posts and no decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three horizontal rails and the diagonal brace clearly, with both posts visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence, with the rails and brace mostly hidden.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, showing the rails and diagonal brace from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin profile and the posts' depth.
